summary: Gruber critiques the redesigned utility app icons in MacOS 26 Tahoe, arguing they represent a significant decline in Apple's design standards. The new icons all use a lazy wrench-and-bolt motif that leaves only about 10 percent of the icon area to identify the app's actual purpose. He details specific failures: Disk Utility shows only an Apple logo instead of anything disk-related, the wrench itself is anatomically wrong, and the AppleScript scroll is improperly rotated. Gruber frames these icons not as a major issue in themselves, but as dead canaries signaling deeper problems with Apple's attention to design craft. He invokes Steve Jobs's famous cabinet-back metaphor to argue these icons reflect a lack of both artistic ability and attention to detail.
key_points: Tahoe's enforced squircle shape combined with the wrench-and-bolt utility motif leaves only ~10% of icon area for app-specific identification
Disk Utility's icon is just an Apple logo inside the wrench, making it impossible to guess the app's purpose from the icon alone
The wrench illustration is anatomically wrong — the jaws are too thin to function as a real wrench, suggesting the designer never used one
The AppleScript Utility icon has its scroll rotated a few degrees off-center from its canonical orientation, a detail error that betrays lack of care
These poorly designed utility icons serve as 'dead canaries' signaling broader decline in Apple's design standards and attention to craft
money_quotes: They all look like placeholder icons made by a developer who would be the first to admit that they're not an artist.
So maybe like 10 percent of the area of the icon is the area where the app can show something that identifies its purpose.
Whoever drew it has obviously never used an open-end wrench because the jaws on the wrench head are way too thin. They'd break off under any significant torque.
If I just showed you this icon without telling you which app it represented, how in the world could you guess what it is?
These are not the work of carpenters who care about the backs of the cabinets they're building.
The whole collection looks like the work from someone with no artistic ability nor an eye for detail. From Apple, of all companies.
The icons in /Applications/Utilities/ in MacOS 26 Tahoe represent a folder full of dead canaries.
key_insight: The shoddy utility app icons in MacOS Tahoe are canaries in a coal mine — individually trivial, but collectively signaling that Apple's legendary attention to design craft and detail is eroding.
